parameters-en.Type: Selects the type of shimmer effect applied. "Luster" is more of a traditional, reverb pedal-type shimmer effect with tighter definition in the lustery bits. "Sheen" is more of a lush, studio plugin-type shimmer effect with a massive, sheeny bloom.
parameters-en.Pitch A: Sets the interval of the first pitch shifter. Set to "Oct Up" for more traditional shimmer sounds; set to "Oct Down" for something a bit creepier. Note that Pitch 1 and Pitch 2 have 0.1 semitone resolution between -1 and +1
parameters-en.Pitch B: Sets the interval of the second pitch shifter. Set to "Oct Up" for more traditional shimmer sounds; set to "Oct Down" for something a bit creepier. Note that Pitch 1 and Pitch 2 have 0.1 semitone resolution between -1 and +1
parameters-en.Intensity: Controls the mix between the pitch-shifted and non-pitch-shifted reverb.
parameters-en.Feedback: Controls how many times the pitch shifting recirculates through the reverb.
parameters-en.Mix: Controls the blend between the reverb and the dry signal. At 0%, no reverb is heard; at 100%, no dry signal is heard.
parameters-en.Pitch Blend: Controls how much of Pitch 1 is heard vs. Pitch 2 (set to "Even" by default).
parameters-en.Decay: Adjusts the decay of the reverb (0.1 sec ~ 45.0 sec or Infinity)
parameters-en.Predelay: Controls the amount of delay heard before the audible onset of reverb. Can sometimes result in more definition between the dry and effected signals.
parameters-en.Room Size: Selects the size of the room (10, 20, or 30 meters).
parameters-en.Damping: Determines the frequency above which the reverb will be absorbed. For example, if your hall is full of people wearing shimmery astronaut costumes, more high frequencies would be absorbed than if the room were empty.
parameters-en.Diffusion: Controls the amount of smearing between discrete echoes, sometimes resulting in a softer effected signal.
parameters-en.Motion: Controls the amount of randomization, which can be helpful to minimize any metallic artifacts common in static reverbs. At higher values, can impart a bit of modulation to the effected signal.
parameters-en.Low Cut: Applies a low cut (or high pass) filter to the reverb, letting you remove the effected signal below a certain frequency.
parameters-en.High Cut: Applies a high cut (or low pass) filter to the reverb, letting you remove the effected signal above a certain frequency
parameters-en.Level: Sets the overall level of the block.
